phase.name: Core API
phase.exit_criteria: Query(), Client, hooks, permissions, MCP tools, and session management are fully implemented and compile cleanly

tasks.description: Implement functional options pattern for configuring Query and Client: WithSystemPrompt, WithMaxTurns, WithCLIPath, WithTransport, WithPermissionMode, WithCanUseTool, WithHooks, WithMCPServers, WithWorkingDir, etc.
tasks.commit: feat(agent): add functional options for configuration
steps: Step 1: Define Options struct with all configurable fields
Step 2: Implement Option type as func(*Options) and create all With* constructor functions
Step 3: Implement defaults resolution (zero-value safe, PATH discovery, default permission mode)
acceptance_criteria: All With* functions compile and configure the Options struct correctly
Zero-value Options produces valid defaults
Options are composable: multiple options can be chained
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented Options struct with all configurable fields (SystemPrompt, MaxTurns, CLIPath, Transport, PermissionMode, CanUseTool, Hooks, MCPServers, WorkingDir), Option type as func(*Options), and all With* constructor functions. NewOptions() applies defaults via applyDefaults() (PATH discovery for CLIPath, PermissionModeDefault). Includes Transporter interface to avoid circular deps with transport/. MCPServerConfig and MCPServerType also defined. 12 tests covering all criteria. Commit: d1d8038

tasks.description: Implement one-shot Query() function that launches CLI, streams messages, and returns iter.Seq2[Message, error] for range-based consumption
tasks.commit: feat(agent): add Query() one-shot streaming function
steps: Step 1: Implement Query(ctx, prompt, ...Option) iter.Seq2[Message, error] signature
Step 2: Wire transport Start + internal parser to yield messages via push iterator
Step 3: Handle context cancellation mid-stream (stop transport, yield context.Canceled)
Step 4: Ensure transport cleanup on iterator exhaustion or early break
acceptance_criteria: for msg, err := range agent.Query(ctx, prompt) { } compiles and yields messages
Context cancellation stops streaming and propagates error
Transport is cleaned up whether iterator is fully consumed or broken early
All message types flow through correctly
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented Query(ctx, prompt, ...Option) iter.Seq2[agent.Message, error] in the root package (package claude) to avoid circular imports (transport and internal/parse both import agent). Uses transport.SubprocessTransport by default or a custom Transporter via WithTransport(). Transport always cleaned up via defer. Context cancellation detected via ctx.Err() after io.EOF. ResultMessage terminates the stream. Requires GOEXPERIMENT=rangefunc with Go 1.22. All 5 tests pass. Commit: 740200b

tasks.description: Implement bidirectional Client supporting multi-turn conversations: create, send queries, receive streaming responses, send follow-ups, interrupt, and close
tasks.commit: feat(agent): add bidirectional Client for multi-turn conversations
steps: Step 1: Define Client struct with persistent transport connection and session state
Step 2: Implement NewClient(ctx, ...Option) (*Client, error) with transport initialization
Step 3: Implement Client.Query(ctx, prompt) iter.Seq2[Message, error] for sending follow-ups
Step 4: Implement Client.Close() for graceful shutdown and concurrency guard (single-session enforcement)
acceptance_criteria: Client supports multiple sequential queries in one session
Concurrent Query calls on same Client return an error
Client.Close() terminates transport gracefully
Client works with custom Transport implementations
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented Client struct (client.go) with sessionID tracking, sync.Mutex-guarded busy/closed/cancelFn fields. NewClient validates CLI path or custom transport. Query() uses context.WithCancel to allow Close() to interrupt in-flight queries; captures ResultMessage.SessionID for --resume on subsequent calls. ErrClientBusy and ErrClientClosed sentinels. 5 tests covering all acceptance criteria. Commit: e137607

tasks.description: Implement hooks system covering all 10 hook events with typed input/output structs and handler function signatures. Events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, ModelResponse, NotificationArrived, Stop, SubagentStarted, SubagentStopped, SessionStarted, SessionStopped, Error
tasks.commit: feat(hooks): add typed hook system for all 10 lifecycle events
steps: Step 1: Create hooks/ package with HookEvent enum and typed Input/Output structs per event
Step 2: Define handler function types: func(ctx, *XxxInput) (*XxxOutput, error) for each event
Step 3: Implement Hooks registry struct that stores handlers and dispatches events
Step 4: Implement hook matchers (tool name patterns, glob matching) for PreToolUse/PostToolUse
acceptance_criteria: All 10 hook events have typed Input and Output structs
Handler signatures provide compile-time type safety
Hooks registry dispatches to correct handler based on event type
PreToolUse hook can modify tool input via output struct
tasks.implementation_notes: Created hooks/ package with HookEvent enum (10 constants), typed Input/Output structs for all 10 events, typed handler function types, and Hooks registry with On*/Dispatch* methods. PreToolUse/PostToolUse use path.Match for glob-based tool name filtering. PreToolUse chaining passes modified ToolInput to subsequent handlers. Updated agent/options.go Hooks field from any to *hooks.Hooks for compile-time safety. 10 tests all pass. Commit: 74c6029

tasks.description: Implement permission management: PermissionMode enum, CanUseTool callback type, permission decision types (Allow/Deny), and permission update propagation
tasks.commit: feat(permission): add permission management types and callbacks
steps: Step 1: Create permission/ package with PermissionMode enum (Default, AcceptEdits, Plan, etc.)
Step 2: Define CanUseToolFunc type: func(toolName string, input map[string]any) (Decision, error)
Step 3: Define Decision type with Allow/Deny variants and reason field
acceptance_criteria: PermissionMode covers all modes from the Python SDK
CanUseToolFunc signature enables custom permission logic
Decision type clearly communicates allow/deny with optional reason
tasks.implementation_notes: Created permission/ package with Mode enum (6 constants: Default, AcceptEdits, Plan, BypassPermissions, DontAsk, Auto), Decision struct with Allow()/Deny() constructors and Allowed()/Reason() accessors, and CanUseToolFunc type returning Decision. agent/options.go updated to alias these types. 4 tests in permission_test.go, agent tests updated for Decision return. Commit: 8387420

tasks.description: Implement MCP tool system: Tool interface (Name, Description, InputSchema, Run), MCP server configuration types (stdio, SSE, HTTP, SDK), and NewMCPServer() constructor for creating tool-serving MCP servers
tasks.commit: feat(mcp): add Tool interface and MCP server configuration
steps: Step 1: Create mcp/ package with Tool interface: Name() string, Description() string, InputSchema() json.RawMessage, Run(ctx, input) (output, error)
Step 2: Define MCP server config types: StdioServerConfig, SSEServerConfig, HTTPServerConfig, SDKServerConfig
Step 3: Implement NewMCPServer(tools ...Tool) that creates an SDK-type MCP server config
Step 4: Implement server config serialization for passing to CLI via options
acceptance_criteria: Tool interface is implementable by user code
All 4 server config types are defined with appropriate fields
NewMCPServer() produces a valid config from Tool implementations
Server configs serialize correctly for CLI consumption
tasks.implementation_notes: Created mcp/ package with Tool interface (Name, Description, InputSchema, Run), ServerConfig interface, and 4 typed config types: StdioServerConfig (command+args+env), SSEServerConfig (url+headers), HTTPServerConfig (url+headers), SDKServerConfig (wraps []Tool). Each type implements ServerType() and MarshalJSON() with CLI-compatible JSON (type field + protocol fields). NewMCPServer(tools ...Tool) returns *SDKServerConfig. 7 tests all pass. Commit: 2bc918d

tasks.description: Implement session management: SessionStore interface, InMemorySessionStore, and standalone functions for List, Get, Fork, Rename, Tag, Delete operations
tasks.commit: feat(session): add session management and SessionStore interface
steps: Step 1: Create session/ package with Session struct and metadata types
Step 2: Define SessionStore interface with CRUD methods
Step 3: Implement InMemorySessionStore as default implementation
Step 4: Implement standalone functions: List, Get, Fork, Rename, Tag, Delete using SessionStore
acceptance_criteria: SessionStore interface enables custom storage backends
InMemorySessionStore passes all CRUD operations
Standalone functions work independently of Client lifecycle
tasks.implementation_notes: Created mcp/ package with Tool interface (Name, Description, InputSchema, Run), ServerConfig interface, and 4 typed config types: StdioServerConfig (command+args+env), SSEServerConfig (url+headers), HTTPServerConfig (url+headers), SDKServerConfig (wraps []Tool). Each type implements ServerType() and MarshalJSON() with CLI-compatible JSON (type field + protocol fields). NewMCPServer(tools ...Tool) returns *SDKServerConfig. 7 tests all pass. Commit: 2bc918d
